createhydraulics.configuration.title: Create: Hydraulics Configs
createhydraulics.ponder.hardened_iron_pi: Hardened Iron Pipes carry hydraulic pressure, measured in Pressure Units (PU).
Joined end to end, they pass that pressure from one pipe to the next, all the way down the line.
Splice in a Flow Gauge to read the live pressure flowing through the line.
createhydraulics.ponder.pressure_valve.t: With no redstone signal, the Pressure Valve stays open and lets pressure flow straight through the pipe beside it.
Give it a redstone signal and it closes, sealing the line so no pressure reaches the far side.
createhydraulics.ponder.flow_gauge.text_: The Flow Gauge splices into a pressure line and shows how much pressure is flowing through it.
Its needle sweeps further around the dial as the pressure on the line rises.
A full dial means 2048 PU, the most a single line can hold. Glance at it to keep your network balanced.
The Aqueduct Intake is an early pressure source. Bury it in water on every side except the top.
Once it is submerged it switches on and pushes 100 PU up into the pipe above it.
Put more intakes on the same line and their output stacks, climbing to 200 PU and higher.
createhydraulics.ponder.hydrostatic_inta: The Hydrostatic Intake reads the water stacked directly above it and sends pressure out of its bottom face.
A single water source is enough to switch it on and feed the pipe below.
The taller the column of water, the more pressure it makes, so build the stack high for a stronger supply.
It reads up to twenty sources and tops out at 600 PU, well beyond what an Aqueduct Intake can reach.
createhydraulics.ponder.hydro_fluid_buck: The Hydro Fluid Bucket carries Hydro Fluid, the working medium of every Hydraulic machine.
Right-click a machine such as the Hydraulic Press to fill its internal tank in a single click.
The Press, Fist and Drill hold it forever. The Hydraulic Motor burns it as fuel, so keep it topped up.
The Hydraulic Press stamps items that rest on a Depot placed right below it.
Set the Press one block above that Depot.
Feed pressure into its top face through a Hardened Iron Pipe.
The line must carry at least 100 PU, supplied by a source such as a submerged Aqueduct Intake.
Load it once with Hydro Fluid, its working medium. The fluid stays inside and is never used up.
Drop a matching item onto the Depot below, here an iron ingot.
Pressured and fuelled, the head drives down and stamps the item into its result, an iron sheet.
It chews through a stack one item per stroke, keeping a slow and steady rhythm.
The Hydraulic Motor turns pressure into rotation to drive your Create machines.
It spins a shaft out of its front face, ready to connect to the rest of your contraption.
Feed pressure into its back face through a Hardened Iron Pipe.
Supply it from a source such as a submerged Aqueduct Intake. The faster you set it, the more pressure it needs.
Load it with Hydro Fluid, which it burns as fuel while it runs, so keep it topped up.
Pressured and fuelled, it spins up to the speed you dial in with the scroll wheel.
The faster it turns, the more rotational force it feeds into the network.
The Hydraulic Drill bores through blocks in the direction it faces.
Feed pressure into its back face through a Hardened Iron Pipe. The line must carry 300 PU, supplied by a source such as a submerged Aqueduct Intake.
Load it once with Hydro Fluid, its working medium. The fluid stays inside and is never used up.
Pressured and fuelled, it snaps the blocks ahead of it, boring a clean tunnel.
Broken blocks drop their loot on the spot. Mount the Drill on a moving contraption and the drops flow straight into the contraption's storage instead.
createhydraulics.ponder.hydraulic_fist.t: The Hydraulic Fist punches straight down and crushes whatever sits below it.
Feed pressure into its top face through a Hardened Iron Pipe. The line must carry 200 PU, supplied by a source such as a submerged Aqueduct Intake.
Load it once with Hydro Fluid, its working medium. The fluid stays inside and is never used up.
Set it over a block it can crush, such as Stone.
Each punch fractures the block in place, turning Stone into Cobblestone.
Punch again to break it down further, Cobblestone into Gravel.
Put a Depot or belt below instead and the Fist crushes the items riding on it.
Each punch steps the stack one tier further down the crushing chain. It never skips a tier — the stack sits there until you punch again.
createhydraulics.ponder.centrifugal_comp: The Centrifugal Compressor turns rotation into hydraulic pressure, a kinetic-powered source for your line.
Drive a shaft into either of its side faces to spin it.
Load it with Hydro Fluid, its working medium. The fluid stays inside and is never used up, but without it the compressor cannot run.
As it spins it draws Stress Units from the network, so it needs a source of rotational force behind it.
In return it pushes pressure up into the Hardened Iron Pipe on its top face.
The faster you drive it, the more Stress it eats and the more pressure it makes.
createhydraulics.ponder.hydraulic_assemb: The Hydraulic Assembly Unit is a fluid-powered universal assembler. Point it at any recipe in the game and it will slowly build it for you.
Pressure enters through its bottom face. Connect a Hardened Iron Pipe there and keep the line at 600 PU or above.
Stack six Aqueduct Intakes on one line, fill a tall Hydrostatic column, or use a Centrifugal Compressor to meet the threshold.
Right-click it with a Hydro Fluid Bucket to fill its tank. The fluid stays inside and is never used up.
Set a filter on any horizontal face to choose the result you want. Drop the matching ingredients in from above or push them in with a funnel.
With pressure, fluid, and the right ingredients it begins assembling — one craft every twenty seconds. The trade-off for making any recipe is patience.
Finished goods collect in its output buffer. Pull them out with a funnel on the side to keep the line moving.
